# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent monitoring misconfiguration.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3'  # Specify the version of docker compose file format (currently set as yml)
services:   # Define services in this section, each service is a separate container instance for your application to run on top of.
    elk_stack:     # Name will be used by CLI command and also Dockerfile/entrypoint if not specified otherwise  (default 'elk-stack')
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch   # Use official ElasticSearch Image from the registry, you can use any other version as well but it must be compatible with Kibana and Logstash in your setup too if not specified otherwise  (default 'docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.12.0')
        environment:   # Define environmental variables for the service here, these will override default ones set by Dockerfile or entrypoint command unless explicitly overridden there in this section too if not specified otherwise  (default 'ELASTIC_PASSWORD=changeme' and ELASTIC_USERNAME=elastic')
            - cluster.name=my-application   # Specify the name of your Elasticsearch Cluster, you can use any other value but it must be unique in a Kibana setup (default 'my-application').  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            - node.name=es01   # Specify the name of your Elasticsearch Node, you can use any other value but it must be unique in a Kibana setup (default 'es01')  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            - discovery.seed_hosts=es02:5397   # Specify the list of hostnames for initial discovering peers in your cluster, you can use any other value but it must be unique (default '[ "localhost" ]')  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            - bootstrap.memory_lock=true   # Enable memory lock for the Elasticsearch instance and disable JVM heap size limit in order to use all available system resources (default 'false'). This is useful when you want your Kibana instances are using more than 1GB of RAM, but only if it's not a shared resource.
            - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms500m -Xmx500m"   # Specify the Java options for Elasticsearch (default '-XX:+UseG1GC')  You should set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
        networks:    # Define network settings in here, these will be used by Docker Compose and can override default ones defined elsewhere too. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'.  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            - my-network   # Specify your network, you may use an existing one by its ID here but it must be defined in docker compose file. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'.  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
        restart: unless-stopped   # Restart services automatically when they stop, you can specify delay in seconds before stopping them too. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'.  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
        deploy:   # Define deployment settings here for your services, these will be used by Docker Compose and can override default ones defined elsewhere too. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'.  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            replicas: 1   # Specify how many instances of your services you want Docker Compose run for. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'. You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
            restart_policy: 
                condition: on-failure   # Specify how Docker Compose restarts your services when they fail. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'. You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise 
            labels:    # Define additional metadata for each instance of a particular service, these will be used by Docker Compose and can override default ones defined elsewhere too. If no name is provided then the label's own key (if any) or 'default'. You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
                - com.example: my-service   # Specify your service, you may use an existing one by its ID here but it must be defined in docker compose file and can't conflict or overlap any other services (default 'com_example=my_services')  You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
    logstash:     # Name will be used by CLI command, you may use an existing one but it must have been defined in docker compose file. If no name is provided then the service's own label (if any) or 'default'. You should also set this to match with elastic/kibana container names if not specified otherwise
        image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash   # Use official LogStash Image from registry, you can use other version as well but it must be compatible with Kibana and Elasticsearch in your setup too (default 'docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:7.12.0')
        networks:    # Define network settings for LogStash here if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('my-network'). You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under networks.
            - my-network   # Specify your network here if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('my_networks') You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under networks.
        restart: unless-stopped   # Restart LogStash automatically when it stops if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('logstash'). You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under restart.
    apm:     # Name will be used by CLI command if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('apm'). You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under networks.
        image: docker.elastic.co/apm/apm   # Use official APM Server Image from registry if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('docker.elastic.co/apm/apm'). You can use other version as well but it must be compatible with Kibana and Elasticsearch in your setup too (default 'docker.elastic.co/apm/apm:7.12.0')
        networks:    # Define network settings for APM here if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('my-network'). You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under networks.
            - my-network   # Specify your network here if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('my_networks') You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under networks.
        restart: unless-stopped   # Restart APM automatically when it stops if no name is provided then the service's own label or default ('apm'). You should also set this to match with elastic and kibana container names (if not specified otherwise)  If you have defined any other services in docker compose file, they must be listed under restart.
